verdicts.reason: It sharply renders a person relaying words from a dead person to a living person.
It identifies the medium as uniquely able to hear and relay a dead person's speech to living people.

verdicts.missing: It lacks a link showing that the words told to people are messages received from the dead person; it carries only a belief in hearing the dead and generic speech.
verdicts.reason: It captures purported hearing of the dead and speaking to people, but not their communicative intermediation.

verdicts.missing: It omits both the living-dead relation and intermediary role, carrying only thinking and saying something to another person.
verdicts.reason: The generic thinker-speaker description fits innumerable unrelated people.
It renders the believed transmission of the same message from a dead person through someone to a living person.
